steps.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Warm, moist air is surging northward across the Plains.
This step uses TV-style temperature markers and keeps the bottom scrubber synced to the METAR timeline.
Use the METAR controls to switch between temperature, dew point, wind, and pressure.

A.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Warm, moist air is surging northward across the Plains.
This step uses TV-style temperature markers and keeps the bottom scrubber synced to the METAR timeline.
Use the METAR controls to switch between temperature, dew point, wind, and pressure.

B.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Explain what is happening.

C.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Analyze the science.

D.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Apply and forecast.

steps.title: Moisture Returns
narrative: This view switches the METAR markers to dew point so moisture return is easier to explain.
The clock and scrubber stay visible here too.

A.title: Moisture Returns
narrative: This view switches the METAR markers to dew point so moisture return is easier to explain.
The clock and scrubber stay visible here too.

narrative: Explain what is happening.

C.title: Moisture Returns
narrative: Analyze the science.

D.title: Moisture Returns
narrative: Apply and forecast.

steps.title: SPC Upgrade
narrative: The Storm Prediction Center highlights the region for organized severe weather.
Forecasters are increasingly concerned about widespread damaging winds.

steps.title: Forecast Temperatures
narrative: Future temperatures should scrub by HRRR frames, matching the way future winds behaves.
State boundaries and county lines remain on for context.

steps.title: Instability Builds
narrative: If a CAPE manifest is present, this step will show it.
If not, this step can be skipped without breaking the demo.

narrative: Forecast wind fields show strong flow feeding the developing system.
This step remains on the HRRR frame scrubber path.

narrative: Initial storms begin to organize into a more connected line.
Radar is now the best tool for tracking rapid evolution.
